dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Dora, don't you pay any attention to what's been happening out there? The rise of the Titan of Time? The siege of Olympus?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Nope. Can't say any of that rings a bell. Olympus, that's where the family members you don't like live, right? And the Titan of Time, I get that he's bad, but he's never done anything to me personally, as far as I know, so...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: This entire encampment exists to resist his efforts to overthrow the gods and rule the world?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Well then! Maybe I should thank him because I happen to like this encampment.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Whoa, hold on, what's going on with you? Never seen you so excited in my life. Death. Whatever. What, did you discover a new species of root vegetable or something?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Dora, I found him... Chronos... and I beat him! Withstood every trick he had, until he yielded and expired.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, nice! So then it's over, right? Or what...?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Well, no, he isn't dead... I mean, he isn't gone. Not yet. I have to go... all right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: You think that you achieved a victory, witch? You merely prolonged your demise. For the Titan whom you vanquished possesses but the smallest fraction of my power!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: So you heard! I'm surprised and humbled that you deigned to pay my meager accomplishments any heed, O Spirit. How did you know?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: I am all-knowing! Also the Shades out there were going off about it. Some of them are right outside the tent, told 'em can't come in, but they're still out there I think...

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: Thank you for keeping them at bay.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Have you ever even spoken with Commander Schelemeus, Dora? Or Odysseus? We have these great masters on our side, who know so much of history. So much they can teach us.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: History? That what they call it nowadays? Well, I don't have big plans to go to war with the rest of you anytime soon, though if I change my mind, I'll probably check in with those guys.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: The renewal project's off to a fine start! How are you managing with all the new responsibility?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Uh OK, I guess! Sometimes I think I'd rather not be doing anything... but, I get that somebody's got to do this stuff, and it's not like any of those slackers out there are gonna do it.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: The other Shades have their own duties. The work needn't be onerous, it can be fulfilling! Helping others, having a sense of purpose...

endLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
endLines.text: My purpose, Mel, is only to hang out.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: I know what you're up to with all this decoration stuff you've got me doing, Mel. What, you think I'm about to find my true calling in death just by updating some old light fixtures?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You already found your calling, as the best haunter in all the Crossroads, if not the entire world. The renewal project is simply a way to give something back to this place.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Maybe... but I'd make a lousy haunter if my existence wasn't utterly devoid of meaning. So don't let me catch you trying to help me find some purpose... 'cause I don't want any.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Sometimes I hardly recognize this place... the tent, I mean. Just look at all this stuff! It's practically a palace now.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I never thought I'd want to live in a palace. Though when you put it that way, I have second thoughts. Are all the latest decorative touches to your liking?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: More stuff to mess with means more haunting opportunities, so yeah! I think it's fine. It's you I'm worried about... not quite as neat and sparse in here as it used to be...

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: We could always neaten it up again.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: You know, back when you got me on your whole Renewal Project thing... I knew what you were up to, Mel. Nothing like a bunch of busywork to make you stop thinking about the pointlessness of your existence, right?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I never saw it as a bunch of busywork. Look at how much we've been able to accomplish here! This place is flourishing, the other Shades seem energized... you had a lot to do with that!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: You really think so...? What I was going to say is, at some point the busywork started to kind of grow on me. It stopped feeling pointless, even if it is.

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: If it doesn't feel pointless, it isn't.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: You got every last one of those Chthonic Chid figures, huh? Never took you for the sort to hoard a bunch of useless stuff.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: They are not useless! I enjoyed collecting them all, and have them neatly organized. And they'll likely grow even more valuable with time!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: What, you planning to sell 'em for profit? I don't see who's gonna take them off your hands! But... I wasn't trying to be mean. I think it's cute you got a bunch of children's toys.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: So are we done? With the Renewal Project. Not much stuff left to get. Though, I guess you could keep switching it around.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I almost didn't realize. Perhaps we are done for the moment with new furnishings and such. Look how far we've come!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Yeah... just chipping away bit by bit, one rug or table at a time, huh? Wow. Let's never do it again, though, I'm beat.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: That's the restless spirit in you talking.

endLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
endLines.text: Then it's time I put 'er to bed.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Those new goods we got in stock... they're from the House of Hades, huh? Quality stuff! No wonder you wanted to save your family.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Dora, for all factors that motivated me as I pursued my task, the opportunity to collect memorabilia from my rightful home was certainly not one of them! I just happen to enjoy it now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey, I'm not judging, and apparently neither are your acquaintances down there. Don't see how come they make you pay with those little gold star stickers though.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I don't know all of the traditions yet either.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Know what? If you keep passing out on that warm-blanket-looking thing over there, it means I finally get the tent all to myself! Good thinking, Mel.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Dora, I'm not merely passing out! It's a way for me to explore my own subconscious through my dreams! And I reserve the right to return to my tent whenever I wish.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Sure. It's not like I was going to kick you out or anything. I'm just saying if you want to keep exploring your subconscious, I'm all for it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Ahh hey Mel, I was just... floating! Floating around, I guess. And existing! Just kind of existing a lot lately. You?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Failing. Making progress, I don't know. Speaking of, shouldn't you be practicing your haunting, Dora?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh! Yeah, no, I thought maybe I'd take a break from that. Why do now what you could do later, right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Oof, you look rough, if we're being honest, Mel. Want to talk about it, or at least tell me the juicy bits because I'm bored?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Last night I visited the Mourning Fields, and... being surrounded by that much despair... I think I must have brought some of it home.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Well why don't I just scare it out of you, you wretched worm?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: You know you can tell me anything, Mel. I won't judge! Well... maybe a little more than the other small green squishy thing you bare your soul to every night, but still.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I never made that particular connection between you and Frinos before. I speak to him about my setbacks and mistakes... but I'm happy to save some for you if you like!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Yep, just lay 'em on me! And the bigger the screw-up, the more I want to know every juicy detail.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: What would you do if you had the tent all to yourself anyway, Dora? You could invite all the other Shades and absolutely trash the place, I suppose...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Wh, what, you mean share it? Mel, not sharing the tent is the entire point, a proper haunting has a one-to-one ratio between the haunted and the hauntee. Even I haven't forgotten the basics.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I can't imagine all you want to do is float around in here, minus the part where we get to speak.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Then you simply lack imagination, friend!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Everlasting pain and suffering upon you, witch! You trespass on my hallowed ground. Your cries for mercy shall drown in your throat!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Please! Forgive my careless indiscretion, madam-spirit, I beg of you! I intended no offense...

endLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
endLines.text: Ho-hoh, wow! You're really into this stuff, huh?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: What would you say is the most frightening thing you've encountered out there lately, Mel? It's important for someone in my position to keep up with the latest on this front.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Well... I'd have to go with the raging infernal beast guarding the only viable way into Tartarus. Absolutely massive, countless sharp fangs, three fire-spitting maws... though he's also my family's dog.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Ahh, you had me going! Well if you're telling me there's nothing scarier out there than a pet dog... it means I've got no competition! Time to rest on these laurels some more.

endLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
endLines.text: All that you see is my domain.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: You, who ventured into my abode, shall suffer an eternity of torment for what you have done! Unless, of course, you are prepared to grant my dying wish...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Forgive my insolence, Spirit, and name your price! I am prepared to do your bidding! Within reason, anyway.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Then swear to me that you shall do my bidding, now! Specifically... I haven't worked out the dying-wish part yet. Ask you to fetch my lover's bones, something like that?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Stand forth, witch! Beyond these thrice-cursed grounds lies only death. Are you prepared to relinquish your own life's blood for that which you seek? Or shall you turn back?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: If I turn back, it's basically a dead end, Spirit. Sounds like I face a dead end either way! I have to check with some of the others, so...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey, the head witch isn't busting your chops too bad, is she? What with you coming back here pretty often lately and all.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I know Headmistress can be very demanding. I'm sure she hopes I would have encountered more success by now, but... she's been nothing but patient.

endLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
endLines.text: Really? Well I hope that sticks!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey is something up? Something's up. You don't have to tell me, but... I mean, you can if you want, I guess.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You can see right through me, can't you...? Someone I care about's in trouble. I have to help.

endLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
endLines.text: Well then don't let me hold you up, get out of here.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Do you remember the name Heracles? The greatest half-god hero of the surface, slayer of the Nemean Lion, and so on?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Nah, not really. But I hear the other Shades go on and on about him every now and then. Why do you ask?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I encountered him. He's as big as they say, that much is sure. He has such a storied past, I thought maybe you'd recall one of the tales.

endLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
endLines.text: Well, you thought wrong, my friend.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Get any sleep at least while you're zonked out back there? Huh, can't even wrap my head around what it must be like for you. Sleeping? They... they say it's like being dead, right? But I'm dead, and I'm always awake.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I think the only one getting good sleep of late is Hypnos, himself. Still hasn't even stirred, no matter what Headmistress has tried.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey, uh, you and that guy, one with the wings, what was his name? Thought he was long dead, though when I saw him here with you, he seemed, shall we say, very much alive? How'd he do that?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You mean Icarus, and did you... were you eavesdropping on what very clearly was a private moment between longtime acquaintances? I thought you were elsewhere!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, no, I was just over there. Didn't want to interrupt. So did you bring him back using a necromantic ritual, or what?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: That's entirely my private business, thanks!

endLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
endLines.text: Trust me you have nothing to be ashamed of.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey, how's the old Ghost Onion working out? Figured you'd make better use of something that helps keep you alive than I can at this point... also it's kind of gross.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It's been very helpful, thank you, Dora. Though your Onion means I'm staying out longer than usual most nights, while you're here on your own...

endLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
endLines.text: I see everything is proceeding as planned...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Not much been going on lately. It's nice! I can look at you and it's like, boom, we had a whole conversation.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Once you're close enough with someone, you often needn't even exchange words to understand their feelings.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Huh. I'm gonna try that out. So not a word if you can help it, OK?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: ...I remember everything... about why I forgot everything... I did something pretty bad, Mel.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Whatever it was, you don't have to tell me. Unless, of course, you wish to... in which case, I'm listening.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Yeah, the thing is I just kind of ruined everything, for everyone. For everyone who's mortal anyway.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: What are you talking about? That couldn't possibly be true!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, imagine there was once this big storage jar, right? Like you'd keep pickles in, except the gods loaded it full of everything that could possibly go wrong. Hate, heartbreak, hair loss, halitosis... you name it.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I'm not following. Why would all mortal vices and failings be placed within this particular jar? It sounds like you may be thinking of some sort of figurative representation.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: No it was a real jar with all that stuff, and I... well, I'm still shaky on the details. Let's chat about it more some other time, I'm beat.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: ...That jar I mentioned, filled with all the bad stuff? I opened it. Wanted to know what was inside! And it went whoosh... I let just about everything out, into the world. Into our lives.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: What? Why would you have done a thing like that? How did you even come across this jar?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: It was a wedding gift, from the Olympians. Prometheus, whom I remember now, interesting guy, he had a hunky brother and we sort of hit it off...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: So the gods wanted you to have a jar full of terrible things. That seems an odd sort of present to give to... who even were you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Eh, just some woman! But there weren't too many of us then, and I don't think the gods liked Prometheus much? So anyway the next time any mortal stubs their toe, pays taxes, gets the sweats, anything like that... it's on me.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Even if what you say is true, you make it sound as though you'd no intention to do it. So it's not your fault.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: The thing is I did do it, so it is my fault... I'm gonna go.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: ...So, yeah, I guess I cursed all mortalkind a while back. So what if I didn't mean it? Intent doesn't matter.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Intent absolutely matters. So then you drank from the River Lethe to rid yourself of the memory of what transpired, to try and rid yourself of the guilt?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Pretty much. But you want to know the one thing I didn't let out of the jar? It was Hope. I was scrambling, and locked it in. The one thing I should have set loose, I didn't.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I'm not so certain. We experience Hope all the time, gods and mortals alike. If you had set it loose... perhaps it would have been smothered by all the other forces, and then what?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Then mortals wouldn't be quite so delusional maybe? Anyway... I think I want to be alone now for a bit. Thanks for talking through it with me, Mel. And making me remember.

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: Are you going to be all right?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Dora, I just can't believe the gods set you up to curse all mortalkind... is there any possibility your memory's at fault?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: ...I guess? But I remember pretty clearly now, even if it was a long while ago. Though the other thing is, don't all you gods go through most of this stuff yourselves? I mean, you don't die like mortals, but... you get the other bad things...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Well, certainly not all of it, but most. So then... perhaps this jar was merely some means of bringing mortals and gods closer together...? To reality, such as we know it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Sounds kind of like the Hope that I left in the jar talking, Mel. You don't think the gods were just... getting back at Prometheus, his brother, and his gorgeous wife?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Perhaps, but... what would they possibly gain from doing such a monstrous thing?

endLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
endLines.text: Gods can be jerks sometimes. Like mortals, but stronger.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: You haven't told anyone about me and the jar, have you? I'd rather keep it between us. Glad I remember now, but... wouldn't want the attention from the details getting out...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I've spoken only to Prometheus of it, for he had knowledge of the circumstances from before. But I won't tell another soul.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Good. Maybe, eventually, I'll go tell everyone myself if I really want to mess with 'em? And even if not, this has given me a ton of new material for my hauntings! It's scary stuff.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Please don't make light of it, Dora. But confronting your past is better than erasing it, I think. For that, you're very brave.

endLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
endLines.text: And you are very foolish! Leave me be.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey, wanted to tell you, thanks for bringing back all of my terrible old memories I tried desperately to erase... I'm serious! I'd rather chew on 'em than spit 'em out.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: As far as I'm concerned, that was your choice more so than mine. I wanted to support you as a friend. We both knew what you would discover had potential to be painful.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Eh, I don't know about painful. That's one of the good things about being a Shade! No more pain! Some rotten thoughts about the way things were, sure... maybe some of those.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I do count that as pain. It's not the same as being struck full-force by Nemesis, but it can hurt more deeply, and the healing process can take longer, by a lot. Though, do you remember more than just the painful memories, at least?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: I guess so, but the other stuff's just not as interesting, you know? Anyway it's all just history. This is me now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Any luck making some magick that'll jog my memory, or what? No pressure, I can wait, but... thought I'd ask!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Incantations involving memory and the past require great care, so it's not something we are going to rush into. And if you have any second thoughts, you tell me right away.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: So, what's the deal with the horned guy, anyway? Saw him and Nemesis chatting like they were an item, or something. It was weird.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: They're siblings, Dora. The horned one is Lord Moros, emissary of the Three Fates. Our distinguished guest, and a valuable ally.

endLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
endLines.text: Yeah! Looks like we really put him up in style...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Wait, so the horned guy over there... he's the one who shows up whenever some mere mortal is about to die in some horrific, unexpected way?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Well, yes. He ensures the will of the Three Fates is done. But don't misjudge him for it, and there's no need to be afraid of him, either.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Are you kidding? I think that's awesome, I... I wonder if he saw me die! Maybe I got crushed by a boulder! Struck by lightning! <Gasp> Murdered by my own lover...! Could you introduce us?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Saw you chatting up Horns again! Yeah, he's a bit of a stiff, but I guess he's not so bad. Think maybe I met him before, back when I died...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Oh I doubt it. You'd remember having met someone like that. And he'd remember you as well! Though, you have been dead a while...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Yeah. Maybe I was one of his first jobs. Just an innocent young woman, doomed to a cruel inevitable future she never deserved...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey if you showed up just a bit sooner, you could have heard Nemesis complaining up a storm again!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Best not mock Retribution Incarnate if you know what's good for you. She and Headmistress have been at each other's throats...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: You know I could, like, possess her for you, right? Just kind of... swoop in there, rattle around in her head a little bit, make her do some bad stuff... <Gasp> embarrassing stuff! Just for a little while, I mean. If I got caught in there...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Whoa, what gives there, Mel? Y-you look like you've seen a... ungh, I don't know, but... whatever it was it got under your skin. <Gasp> Oh! Skin seems nice!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I'm all right, just a little shaken. First nightmare since all this started.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: About time, you were past due. Oh, how scary would you say it was, compared to, um, the terrifying visage that you see before you now?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Wonder if I ever used to have nightmares... so, so you can just... imagine yourself in these completely different horrible situations?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Yes. And sometimes I'm not even there, which can make them even more unsettling. Though they don't trouble me as much anymore...

endLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
endLines.text: Well... at least you've got me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Seems like you've been sleeping better lately. Not that I like watching you intently while you're out cold.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Lately my dreams haven't troubled me as much for whatever reason. That's probably why. You... truly watch me sleep?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey, you're up. You were out a while. See anything good down there that time?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I fought past Erebus, down into Oceanus. It was so strange, being underneath the sea. Do you remember the sea, Dora?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: The sea... the sea, the sea, the sea, that's the thing with all the water in it, right? Heard it's nice sometimes! Also kills a bunch of people, death by drowning, so — I've been getting mixed messages, I guess.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: I try not to listen to what the Shades out there are warbling about, but they make it sound like lately you've been through some pretty hairy times?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It's hard to know exactly what they're referring to, but... I've managed to reach Mount Olympus, for one thing. Never thought I'd see it. The fighting there is very fierce...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Olympus... where the other members of your family have all been hanging out! What are they fighting over now?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Their reign over the heavens and the Earth, and the continued existence of their home.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It's been nice to see you out and about a little more often lately, not just cooped up in here. How are you managing?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Eh, I'm managing. Those other Shades don't know the first thing about haunting, and it turns out they've got the dirt on everybody here... including you.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Comforting to know the brunt of our resistance is enjoying themselves a good bit of gossip.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey, what happened, you look weird or something, I don't know. Weren't out too long. Took someone to the woods...?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: No, Dora, though I don't think I can sufficiently explain. You know the Pitch-Black Stone? Turns out it can... do things.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: The purple rock can do things, you say. Know what? I don't even want to know the rest, just gonna let my imagination take over for you, thanks.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: You've been staring at that picture more and more. Happy family! Well it's starting to get to me, because I think it's starting to get to you. Don't get all misty on me, Mel.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Thank you, Dora. Though you needn't worry. I won't get lost in memories because there isn't much for me to remember. I just wonder what my brother and my birthparents were like.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Uh-huh! Sounds awful lot like dwelling on the past to me. Nothing you could have done! Hey... you could be doing nothing right now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: So hold on, how is it you're going after the Titan of Time way down below, but he's also attacking Olympus way up top? Is he two separate guys or just the one?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Chronos isn't attacking Olympus personally, Dora. He's just sending his legions there. He has a dangerous commander leading the assault... a different Titan called Prometheus.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: A different Titan called... Prometheus...? Prometheus... huh...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Yes, the Titan of Foresight. Even you probably know the tale of his punishment for stealing from the gods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: No, it's... I think... ah, don't worry about it. See you later Mel!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: So hold on, how is it you're going after the Titan of Time way down below, but he's also attacking Olympus way up top? Is he two separate guys or just the one?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Well it's not really him I'm going after anymore. But in any case, Chronos appointed someone else to lead the assault on Olympus... a different Titan called Prometheus.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: A different Titan called... Prometheus...? Prometheus... huh...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Yes, the Titan of Foresight. Even you probably know the tale of his punishment for stealing from the gods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: No, it's... I think... ah, don't worry about it. See you later Mel!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Hey is everything all right with you, Dora? I didn't mean to upset you with talks of Titans when last we spoke.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Ahh I'm good, Mel. I don't get mad much anymore really, and don't get weirded out either, it's just... something about the name Prometheus? It's like... it meant something to me...?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: But, Prometheus was imprisoned for aeons prior to this point. If you remember him... it's from a while back.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: I'd be ancient history! But, I hardly remember yesterday, don't know how come I'd remember something from that long ago. Not that I do remember anything from then... except...

endLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
endLines.text: Ah, just a feeling... but, it isn't great.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Dora, I hope you don't mind, but I asked Prometheus himself about this old connection that you feel. He's cagey with his words, but does seem to remember you, in turn.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh! Kind of weird of you to do that especially since it sounds like he's trying to kill your family up there, but thanks! What'd he say?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Well, he said... he's not to blame for mortalkind's failings and such, and that... neither are you. And asked I tell you so, on his behalf. Before attacking me per usual, of course.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Whew, that's a relief! All this time, I figured if there's any two individuals responsible for all the badness mortals have to deal with, it's Prometheus and me! We're talking stomach aches, heartaches, taxation, lying, cheating... you name it.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It's utter nonsense, but... do you have any idea what he could have meant?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Nope! But since you're the one who's talking to him on the regular, maybe you could ask. Now that we're curious.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Prometheus refuses to elaborate about your past with him, Dora. But he believes you're going to remember soon enough. My worry is... you're better off not knowing.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: And, what makes you say that? Besides the part where I've already told you I get a not-great feeling just from sounding out his name? Prometheus... eugh, yeah! There it is!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: He gave me the impression you forgot about him, and much more, by choice. If that's true, perhaps it's for the best. If only for the sake of your past self.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Forget my past self, Mel, my current self is pretty caught up now in getting to the bottom of all this! Isn't there some sort of witch-spell you could do to jog my memory, or something?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Well... now that I know of this specific connection you seem to have, it's something to work with. But, are you absolutely sure it's what you want?

endLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
endLines.text: Oh yeah! It's gonna be something good...

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: I'll take your word for it...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Mel! Thought I finally had the place to myself, then next I know, you're back. Damn near scared me to life. Rough night again?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Rough night, Dora. Fear not, you'll have the tent to yourself yet! See you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: You know, it's kind of spooky how you never come in through the front flap anymore. Ever think of taking up haunting, yourself?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Oh, yes! I figure if none of this works out, the lot of us can wander the earth, striking fear into the hearts of any mortals who dare look upon us.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Whew! Thought you'd never snap out of it. You OK?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Yes, thank you, Dora. I hope I didn't cause you any concern. Have you been by my side here all this time?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: What, me? No. Just out for a breather.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Don't you have friends among the other Shades, Dora? You could join them for some activity out there. It's picked up a lot of late.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Foolish witch! Haunting this tent is a full-time obsession. We lesser Shades can be a little single-minded! I guess kind of like mortals.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: How'd you get all those other Shades out there to do the busywork around here, anyway? They practically worship the ground you walk on.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Headmistress took them in. I helped train and communicate with them. Many came from being trapped between the realms. Some never got a proper burial. Others died unfulfilled, or violently. They never made it to the Underworld.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Oof... that's rough. Say, you don't think I fall into one of those categories, there, do you? I'd hate to think I didn't get a proper burial.

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: It's not a laughing matter, Dora.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Getting crowded out there! More Shades, guy with the horns. I don't like it, Mel! Not one bit.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: As we grow in numbers, we grow in strength. What's your concern with that?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't like to share. Except this tent, I don't mind sharing it with you! Most of the time.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Dora! Wow! What brings you all the way out here? The taverna's just about the last place I expected to find you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Um, way to make me feel incredibly self-conscious, thanks, Mel! But, yeah I'm, I'm trying to put myself out there a bit more, I guess...!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Well I think that's great. Though, I know how difficult it is. I used to never be able to approach others, but I kept doing it, and... it's easier now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Easier, but... never easy, right? Ehh, don't know why I bother... most of these Shades are pretty boring.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Dora! Wow! What brings you all the way out here? The taverna's just about the last place I expected to find you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Um, way to make me feel incredibly self-conscious, thanks, Mel! But, yeah I'm, I'm trying to put myself out there a bit more, I guess...! Now that you've saddled me with my big important task.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Well I think that's great. Though, I know how difficult it is. I used to never be able to approach others, but I kept doing it, and... it's easier now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Easier, but... never easy, right? Ehh, don't know why I bother... most of these Shades are pretty boring.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey Mel! The good Commander there has offered to train me as one of his disciples. It's a big decision, so I'm kind of mulling it over...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I've learned so much from him, Dora. Headmistress taught me my craft. But Commander Schelemeus taught me... erm...

endLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
endLines.text: How to beat up an old man...?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: So what did you decide, when it comes to the Commander's offer to train you as one of his disciples? Perhaps you'd make a great warrior.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: You're probably right, it's just I kind of have a lot going on right now? For one thing, I follow you around constantly. I don't want to bite off more than I can chew.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Restraint requires discipline. You know, you've come a long way from the listless Dora I recall. Even without the Commander's training.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Foolish witch! Speak no more of this again, lest I curse you and your line for two eternities, not merely one!

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: I regret my every word, Spirit.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey meant to ask you, what is with those statues in the training grounds? Are those supposed to be you, because... well... they're a lot bigger than you...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: ...Statues sometimes portray their subjects in an unnaturally flattering or dramatic light! But in any case, I earned the title of Night's Champion, and... those were my reward.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: ...I'll never look at you the same way again! I really like the one with the huge axe.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You must remember something of life on the surface, right, Dora? Anything at all? I wonder what it must have been like.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: I don't remember, and I don't wonder! Who cares? This is the life, right here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey, you were talking in your sleep a little bit. Not that I paid too close attention, but... where were you last night, anyway? You seem a little off.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Oh, just... I was up there, on the surface. I never thought I'd see it for myself, and now I'm there night after night. It's cold, damp... overrun with the dead.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: You're really selling me on the whole thing. If it's that bad, how come you keep on going over there? I'd just stay here if I were you... or me.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You don't understand. I have to go that way. It's part of my task. Well, not officially, but... look I won't bore you with the details. I should go.

endLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
endLines.text: Have fun being all cold and damp.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Ever think maybe we're just not cut out for this, Mel? Me with the haunting, you with the... what're you trying to do, again?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Find the Titan of Time. Punish him for his crimes against my family. Restore the rightful ruler of the Underworld.... Hey, you're cut out for haunting, Dora!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Aw, thanks! Just like I'm sure you're cut out for all that other stuff.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: So what do you even do when you're out, collect more plants and stuff like that? You used to stay in all the time.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Dora, I was training in secrecy all that time! While I'm out each night, mostly I have to fight my way to Chronos... past a variety of things that don't like having me around.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Can't imagine why. And you just keep trying to do the same thing, huh. I would've given up so long ago! You should try it. Giving up.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Dora, couldn't help but notice that my jar collection wasn't sorted properly. Are you trying to subtly drive me mad?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: No.... Although, now that you put it that way, maybe in the future, yes. I was working on minor possessions and your mushroom bottle kind of slipped. So I put it back!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You put it incorrectly back. I... augh. Just tell me of such things next time, would you?

endLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
endLines.text: Maybe I will, maybe I won't.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: You know, Mel, you can be a bit uptight sometimes. Where'd you even get that from? Even the head witch is more relaxed than you.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Look I'm under a lot of pressure right now, all right? Everybody's counting on me to literally kill Time... so I would like this tent to be an orderly place! Seeing as I'm having to return so often...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Eh, so what? Killing time's my specialty, don't see me gettin' all worked up.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Dora, I was thinking... if I promise not to be so uptight, as you call it... would you promise not to move my belongings about when I'm away?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: How dare you make demands of me, you insignificant fleck? Mmm, nope, no deal. Want to be uptight, go ahead, don't stop on my account.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You just want to keep messing with my stuff!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey where've you been? You smell! Not in a bad way necessarily, and I don't know how I can smell, or remember any smell, so... that is weird.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I was up top. Must be the sea air and the salt, distinct from Oceanus down below. I sailed across the waters, toward Olympus, on a ship!

endLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
endLines.text: Exciting! I was right here the whole time.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: You know, it's kind of nice out here, considering we're not in a tent... quieter than the taverna, and those disciple-Shades by the Commander worship the ground I walk on.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I'm glad to hear that you've grown comfortable in these training grounds. Though as for the Commander's disciples, you haven't frightened them or anything, have you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Ah, let's just say they know I can reduce them to the essence of agony itself, should they so much as dare to challenge me!

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: Please don't make a negative impression...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: So, how's it going on Olympus recently? Or wherever you were last night. See anything good up there besides the snow? Not sure how come I know about snow...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I made it all the way to the summit, where I confronted the ancient monster called Typhon. It's said that all other monsters can be traced directly to him... but, he's by far the biggest and the worst I've ever seen.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Mel, I'm the one who's supposed to scare you, not the other way around. So, did you beat this guy, or what?

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: Erm... not quite. But I intend to.

endLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
endLines.text: Well let me know if I can help!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: So, how's it going on Olympus recently? Or wherever you were last night. See anything good up there besides the snow? Not sure how come I know about snow...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I made it all the way to the summit, where I confronted the ancient monster called Typhon. It's said that all other monsters can be traced directly to him... but, he's by far the biggest and the worst I've ever seen.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Mel, I'm the one who's supposed to scare you, not the other way around. So, did you beat this guy, or what?

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: As a matter of fact, I did!

endLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
endLines.text: Well then he's not so scary after all.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey, you look awfully... something. And kind of in a hurry, but I want to know what happened if you don't mind telling me right quick. Though I reserve the right to not care.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: The monster Typhon... last night I destroyed him. As in there's nothing left of him. We still have Chronos to contend with, but... Olympus is no longer in imminent peril. And we have the upper hand.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh! Well, that's great, isn't it? Sounds like your big important task is almost done! That'll be a real load off, right?

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: We'll see... I have to go.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Say Dora? Would you mind accompanying me over to the baths? I'd hate to think they aren't haunted.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Do I want to float aimlessly in some near-boiling water? Do I ever! What are we waiting for?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Wonder if I was ever shy about this kind of stuff...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You still can sense something of the waters' warmth, can't you, Dora?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, I can see all the steam rising, which I associate with warmth, so... I guess it kind of tricks me into thinking warm thoughts.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: That doesn't sound so bad. Though, you do remember what warmth was like, then? From your mortal days?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Ohh, not really, no. I, I guess I've just heard so much about it, it's like I've experienced it myself.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Whew! That was basically like floating around in your tent, though I appreciate it nonetheless!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Hey I've got these bath salts here that are more than enough for me, if you're not busy at the moment...?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Mel, you know I like doing nothing. Sitting in the hot springs with you? Well that's got to be the next best thing so, shall we?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Like being cooked in a cauldron, isn't it?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Don't you ever wonder how you used to be, Dora? When you were flesh and blood? I bet you were very beautiful.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Hah! Whaaat, seriously? Is it because of my voice or because of my curves? Nah, you're just messing with me.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I am not! It's because of your confidence, I suppose. You're very self-assured! Some Shades cower before me, but you and I always got on.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, some Shades are total buffoons. Anyway, it doesn't really matter to me how I used to be. If I was happy about it, I figure I'd remember.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You think you've deliberately forgotten your past?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Yeah. Why else would I have forgotten everything? Probably took a couple swigs from the River Lethe, and that was that! Clean slate.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: The River of Forgetfulness... you think you were down in Elysium?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: You know, if I'm not mistaken I think I had a good time with you back there. Thanks, Mel!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Forget about the Nectar and Ambrosia, what if we go to the springs again? It's quiet there. Seems more your style... and for that matter, mine.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: I was planning to say no to whatever you had in mind just to mess with you, but the springs do sound OK. So let's go.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Aren't you angry with the gods...? For what they did to you, and all of mortalkind, apparently. So what if it was a long time ago! The more I think about it, the angrier I get...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: I mean, honestly, it was a pretty good prank! But, it was nasty, too. I don't know which one of you all was responsible, but don't really care. Not big on the whole anger thing.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You're too kind... more than my family at least. I don't think there's any taking back what happened, but... for what it's worth, once all this is over, I'm going to do all I can to make it so we gods act more decently with mortals from now on.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Speaking on behalf of all mortals who ever lived or ever will, I think we'd mostly like it if you left us alone. Might be a lot of evils in this world, but... we have ways of figuring things out.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: ...Can you and I remain as friends, at least?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Well let me think about it for a little while and get back to you, all right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Done a lot of thinking, and we're good to go on that remain-as-friends thing, Mel. Though I'll keep you posted if anything comes up!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Would you be interested in visiting the springs with me, Dora? A warm soak can be good for the soul.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Nah. Kind of busy doing nothing whatsoever at the moment, though I might be up for a soak some other time.

options.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
options.text: This night is pretty full, though I think I can squeeze that in.

options.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
options.text: Well if we've both got time to kill, then I guess why not.

options.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
options.text: Huh. Sure. The springs are one of my favorite places to just float.

options.speaker: PlayerUnit
options.text: ...This is nice...

options.speaker: PlayerUnit
options.text: ...Could stay here all night...

options.speaker: PlayerUnit
options.text: ...Let our worries melt away...

options.speaker: PlayerUnit
options.text: ...I needed this...

options.speaker: PlayerUnit
options.text: ...Whew, it's been a time...

options.speaker: PlayerUnit
options.text: ...Warmer than usual tonight...

options.speaker: PlayerUnit
options.text: ...So peaceful here...

options.speaker: PlayerUnit
options.text: ...Just what I needed...

options.speaker: PlayerUnit
options.text: ...Whew, this is good...

options.speaker: PlayerUnit
options.text: ...Always pleasant...

options.speaker: PlayerUnit
options.text: ...<Sigh> It's nice here...

options.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
options.text: ...Being here sure brings back memories, you know? Mainly of the last time.

options.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
options.text: ...Sometimes I think this even beats floating around in the tent. Maybe I should haunt this place instead...

options.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
options.text: ...I guess the upside of a bunch of aching muscles is you get to soothe them sitting in this thing.

options.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
options.text: This is the part where I'm supposed to feel more refreshed or something, right?

options.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
options.text: Good times back there, Mel. Thanks for bringing me along.

options.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
options.text: Now that was a good stretch of pointlessly existing back there.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: I'll keep an eye on all your stuff while you're away.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Good to chat, but I better get back to haunting.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Got a lot of weird stuff in here, you know that?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Just haunting up the place. Don't worry about me...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey, Mel. I mean, Ravens shall feast on your eyes!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey, Mel. I mean, In seeking me you seek only your doom!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Disturb me thus and I shall torment you for three eternities!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: May your days be filled with dread, your nights with fear!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: A thousand curses on you for invoking me!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Do not presume to tarry o'erlong within my tent, witch!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Do what you got to do. I'm not even here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Juuust floatin' around, don't mind me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Feel free to stay in your own tent as long as you like.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Let me know if there's somebody I can torment for you, OK?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Hope your big task thing's been going all right!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: I'll try not to muck everything up while you're out.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Uhh, can I get a little privacy, here, Mel?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Sooner you get out of here, sooner I can go through all your stuff.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: You exist, I exist... could be worse, right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Have a good whatever time it is doing whatever it is you do!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Go knock 'em dead for me, or back to life! Whatever's worse.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm going to haunt this tent so bad once you get out of here...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Just go with the flow out there! It's what I do!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Damn, Mel! Wow. If only looks could kill, am I right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Hope nothing horrible happens to you out there!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Mel, you've inspired me... to float right here, for the time being.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey, Mel! I mean, You hold no sway over me, witch!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Your incessant reappearances have come to be acceptable, witch!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Think maybe there's anything we ought to change around in terms of furnishings and all that?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Sometimes I just want to, like, possess someone, you know? Only for a little bit.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't mind me! Just doing my thing over here, Mel.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Those Shades out there been saying you did good again tonight I guess?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Want anyone around here tormented for a little while, just give me the signal.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: You deign to look upon me, witch? I shall haunt your every waking moment!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Those who gaze into my eyes are forever doomed to suffer as I did!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: An eternity of suffering and insect-bites await all those who dare to cross my path.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Been keeping an eye on things while you're away, and terrifying anyone who dares approach!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: I'll be keeping an eye on the tent for you while you're out all night, Mel.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Need anything from me, I'll be right here. Or one of the other places you go.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: One of the other Shades said hi to me earlier, for the first and final time!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Everything's been good enough here lately, long as you can say the same.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Unnamed_01
dialogueLines.text: Who dares disturb my eternal rest? You trifle with powers far beyond your comprehension, witch!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: That was pretty decent, right, Mel? Maybe pushing it, calling you witch like that.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Solid haunting all in all, Dora, and I thought the witch part worked. But, I'd better go. Something I have to do.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Hey, if you're not busy, up for a little visit with me to the riverside? I've got a twin lure we could use right here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't know that I'll be doing you much good, between my inability to use a rod of fishing and my inability to keep quiet, but hey! You asked for it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: The River of Lamentation. Nothing but a bunch of sad stuff floating around in there. Sure you wouldn't rather leave it be?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Whatever's wallowing in there is going to stay put, unless it's finally decided it's had enough. For any such souls, we'll be doing them a favor, I think.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Not like we owe them, and not like they'll do us any favors back. And if all they can do now is feel sad for themselves at the bottom of a mucky river, fishing them out might not do them any good anyway.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: We're not fishing them out, really. It's more like we're giving them an opportunity to escape their circumstances. It's a bit different from the mortal way of fishing in that sense.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Then if they don't like it any better up here, that's on them...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, that definitely was a thing that happened. Thanks!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: What would you say to a brief trip to the fishing pier with me? It's nice and quiet over there!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Eh, don't really want to move right now. And those fish would probably scare way too easily for a haunter of my skill.

options.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
options.text: Need a fishing partner? Say no more. I am completely in.

options.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
options.text: Sure, I was just thinking why not pay a visit to the riverside again. Not that I'd go unless you asked.

options.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
options.text: All of the river's denizens shall come to know true fear!

options.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
options.text: Oh hey, turns out there's a gap smack in the middle of my busy schedule right now! Lead the way.

options.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
options.text: Even with no hands, I refuse to come back empty-handed after all that.

options.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
options.text: Did way too much thinking back there, but at least we got this.

options.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
options.text: Weird how it's possible to do something and nothing at the exact same time.

options.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
options.text: You know, all in all, that was kind of OK! Thanks, Mel.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Wanted you to have this, Dora. To the best haunter in all the Crossroads!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: See, I knew you were going to pull this gift-custom thing on me sooner or later, so I have come prepared. Catch!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, you don't need to get this kind of stuff for me! I can just take whatever stuff of yours I want when you're out!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Well you can't take this while I'm out! I keep it on me at all times, for just such an occasion.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Fool! I am not susceptible to mere baubles such as this! Ah, just kidding. Totally susceptible. Gimme!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: At last, I have discovered a vulnerable point in your defenses!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Starting to get the feeling maybe I liked this stuff back when I had eyeballs and so on.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Doesn't matter whether you liked it then, what matters is you like it now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Ungh, on the one hand, I really don't know what I did to deserve this. But on the other hand, I really don't care! It's mine now.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It most certainly is! And there's more in it for you if you keep on haunting this tent!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Such pitiable offerings as this are hardly worth my notice, witch! No, but, seriously, thanks. Nobody ever gets me anything! Besides you, I mean. It's nice, you know?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It's nice for me as well! Having you here as company means a lot, so it really is the least I could do.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey, cool it with the Nectar, would you, Mel? Ever since we tried Ambrosia, I've been cultivating a much more sophisticated palate, for a Shade. No more cheap stuff.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Dora, the Nectar of the gods can hardly be considered cheap! Most mortals go through the entirety of their existence without so much as a taste! And you don't like it?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: You know, I do, but it's a little on the sweet side for me? Though, I appreciate you treating me like royalty... like a spirit of unfathomable power...!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I've met many great and noble Shades, Dora, though I can confidently say that none of them are anything like you. This Ambrosia is yours; shall we open it at the taverna?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: I shall accept your offering this time, witch. But I'd rather hang onto it for now. Unless you want to have it with me here? I just don't want to go anywhere is all.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Oh! Of course. Here, the aroma alone should do most of the work. To your continued hauntings!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Thanks. This stuff is definitely pretty good. How come you have both Nectar and Ambrosia, anyway? It's weird. Two different godly beverages, only one's an even bigger deal.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: They're suitable for different occasions. It would never do to offer Ambrosia to a newer acquaintance, for example. It's reserved mainly for feasting between close friends or lovers.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Well that sounds just like us! But, I think I might be over this stuff too...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Dora, I've a favor, if you're not too busy. My task means I no longer can oversee the renewal project around here. Would you attend to it?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Wait, you're asking me to watch over the total transformation of this entire encampment into some ultimate haven for Shades and outcasts?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I know I can count on you. I'll provide the materials, you'll be the main point of contact.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Not going to lie, Mel, sometimes I wish I had a big important task. Not that floating isn't one.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You know my stance on this, Dora. You need to get out of this tent. You're very talented! I've never met a Shade that speaks like you!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Ooh, I can talk, makes me so special. Tsch! I'm sure any lowly Shade could do it after being stuck here listening to you people long enough.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Everything all right, Dora? Any new hauntings need evaluation or anything?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Nah! I — I'm — I'm fine, it's just... think I've hit a bit of a slump is all. Happens, right? I'll get over it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey, if I were you I'd... well, come to think of it, I'd... I'd do a lot of things. Go for a jog! That seems nice. Having legs! Getting the heart rate going, a heart seems nice!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Stop it, Dora. I don't like it when you get like this.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Make something with my hands! I don't know, a cup? A bowl. I'd eat! Eating seems nice. Or even just remembering what that was like...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Hey Dora, what if hypothetically there were to be the dawning of another age at some point, and the gods then truly did leave mortals more or less alone? More than before.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: What are you making me some kind of offer here? I know you're a big deal lately but not even you have the power to pull something like that off, Mel. Why would the gods give up their gig?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Even the gods are subject to the will of the Three Fates. None of us can entirely control what shall come to pass, and there are prophecies that cannot change no matter what.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: You really believe that stuff, even after everything you did? I mean, was there really some kind of prophecy about Chronos, and how you'd end up beating him eventually, once you realized your true potential thanks to me?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Well, no, because the Fates went missing throughout most of this ordeal, but... they don't tend to be mistaken.

endLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
endLines.text: Right. I'll believe it when it happens.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey so what exactly happened around here? Did you complete your big important task? Because that's what I'm hearing from the other Shades.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Well, for the most part, yes! My family is back! My mother and father are restored to the Underworld throne, and my brother's back too! But we have many details to sort out, and... the Titan Chronos is going to be assisting us with that.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: The Titan you said you were going to slay this whole time... assisting us. Hate to say it, Mel, but sounds to me like you totally blew it. At least the Titan part.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: He's no longer himself, or rather... no longer the way he used to be. And we'll be closely watching him.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Sure. So then... congratulations are in order, basically?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Got a new picture there I see. That from your mom and dad? Looks newer than the other one. You're all grown up!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It was a bit of a parting gift, considering I won't be staying at the House of Hades with the rest of them. Though I'll be seeing them around, I think.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Oof, harsh! How come they didn't want you living in their subterranean stronghold with them? You're their kid!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: They did want me to stay, it's just... my home is here. They're my family, but so is this.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: What have you lost your mind? Wait, I'm your family too, that's what you're saying?

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: Sure, if you're up for it.

endLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
endLines.text: Oh all right, I guess why not?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Gah! A scourge upon your bloodline for re-summoning me, witch! Hey, uh, everything OK, there, Mel?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Oh, yes Dora, it's just... I should have gone farther that time. I'll give more notes on your haunting later, all right?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Hey, now that the taverna's back, how about I treat you to a bit of the Ambrosia of the gods? You have my word it's good!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, I guess I'm not in the middle of anything, and since you did give me your word... know what, I'm in.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Considered a delicacy, that figures. Kind of a weird consistency, are you supposed to drink this stuff or eat it?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You're mainly just supposed to savor it with friends. I agree it's a bit unusual, but then, it's intended for extraordinary circumstances.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Yeah? What makes this so extraordinary? We're just... lounging around. Not in the tent, which granted isn't normal in my case, but... still.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It's ours to decide what's special, don't you think? Yes, we see each other night in and night out. But we have never shared a fancy bottle like this, just the two of us.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: I guess I see your point, but... bit of a paradox or something, right? Ambrosia's meant for extraordinary events, but it's the Ambrosia itself making things... unnaturally festive.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Supernaturally, even. My skin, it's beginning to crawl, as though... something is here with us! Oh, gods...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: The gods themselves shall quake with fear, the likes of which your frail consciousness shall never comprehend!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: ...All right so then Ambrosia's not that bad. I got to go unwind, but... that was fun.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Want to join me at the Taverna for a bottle of Ambrosia? I enjoyed our previous outing.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: I enjoyed it too but it was kind of a lot, being in that crowd... still a little drained from last time, I think.

options.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
options.text: I wouldn't go to crowded places with just anybody, but with you, Mel? Sure.

options.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
options.text: Don't see what makes this night Ambrosia-bottle-worthy, but hey, if you insist!

options.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
options.text: You dare make such demands of me, witch? Though sure, let's go, why not.

options.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
options.text: ...I never used to think I'd fit in around here. But look at this place... seems like I fit in completely fine.

options.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
options.text: ...Pour your offering to me, witch, and I shall decide whether to accept it or condemn you to a thousand painful deaths!

options.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
options.text: ...Shades from all over the place in here. Don't you dare introduce us. I like having an air of mystery!

options.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
options.text: ...That's more than enough being out in public for me for a little while, thanks! Though it was nice.

options.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
options.text: ...Thanks, Mel! Scary to think the taverna's started growing on me a little bit...

options.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
options.text: ...Well, that wasn't as bad as I thought it was going to be, and I didn't even think it'd be that bad!

options.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
options.text: ...I guess that was a little different from just being in the old tent...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: ...And so I say, begone accursed witch! I have occupied this grove for aeons, long before your pitiable tent came to rest upon it! Oh hey Mel.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I see the two of you have met! Lord Moros, Dora speaks the truth as always. She's been here longer than I have, if you can believe it!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: I can, indeed. For I know an old soul when I see one. It's a pleasure to meet you, Dora. Do let me know if I can be of service in some way.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Haha. Count on it, Horns! Now then, I bid your leave. Farewell!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: ...His own brother? Eugh, no way, that is twisted! Your sisters are sick!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: Well... they have many mortal lives to untangle, so to speak. The work can grow repetitive, lose its spark. So they liven things up from time to time.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh totally I, I would completely do the same if I were them. I wonder what they did to me.... Could you, like, look me up, or something...?

endLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
endLines.text: Heh! I fear I'm not at liberty to do such things.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Now kneel before me, O Horned King, and know your place! Lest I reduce you and that flowing mane of yours to dust and scatter it to the four winds!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Yes, that was truly excellent, Dora. Although, Horned King? That made it sound as though I was important in some way, when I think you were going for sheer dominance.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: Yeah, that's a good note. Just kind of popped into my head, you know? The antlers... they are striking! They real?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dora_01
dialogueLines.text: ...So you basically just stand here, saying stuff, whatever pops in your head, and those other Shades, and Mel... they eat it up?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: They do eat it up, Disciple, for it is nourishment borne of the wisdom of a vast experience, well-informing that which pops into my head! Now could you please give me a break...?
